title: cli: file_transfers HashMap unbounded; hostile peer can exhaust heap with open FileHeaders
description: The file_transfers HashMap in the CLI's chat loop has no upper bound on the number of concurrent in-flight transfers. A hostile authenticated peer can send N FileHeader messages with distinct transfer_ids (each accepted if size_bytes <= 10 MB) without sending any FileChunk messages. Each entry allocates a Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>> of up to FILE_MAX_CHUNKS=230 Nones. With no eviction policy and no maximum entry count, this allows heap exhaustion proportional to the number of open connections the attacker maintains. Fix: cap the map size (e.g., 64 entries) and drop the oldest or reject new FileHeaders beyond the cap.
